_comment: v1 additions to .claude/project.json. Merge these keys into your existing project.json (the v0.1 keys are documented in ../workflow/project.example.json). Every v1 key is optional — defaults are documented below. The v1 mission orchestrator and its subagents read this file via the same mechanism as v0.1 (see workflow/agents/spec-translator.md step 1).
_mission_root_comment: Where missions/<id>/ directories live. Relative to repo root. Default: 'missions/'.
_mission_model_seats_comment: Per-role model assignment. The orchestrator runs hot (opus); workers and validators run on sonnet; the memory broker is a thin shim on haiku. Override if you want to experiment with model-per-seat diversity (e.g., a different family for the validator).
_mission_caps_comment: Hard caps and pacing knobs. Dispatch caps are observable and deterministic; cost is not (the orchestrator cannot introspect its session spend), so we cap dispatches as the cost proxy. Wake intervals: active 270s sits under the 5-min prompt-cache TTL so the cache stays warm; idle 1500s amortizes one cache miss over ~25 min of waiting.
_memory_comment: Cross-mission memory. Local mode writes markdown under <local_root>/missions/ and indexes via <local_root>/index.json. Mem0 mode (opt-in) mirrors writes to https://api.mem0.ai with Bearer auth from the env var named in api_key_env. Mem0 is augmentation, not replacement — local always writes. The memory-broker is the ONLY agent that reads these settings.
_mission_user_test_comment: Configures the user-flow-tester subagent. preview_url_command is a shell command that prints a URL to stdout — the orchestrator runs it before dispatching user-flow-tester and passes the captured URL. The command is responsible for the URL being reachable: either the dev server is already running ('echo http://localhost:5173' is fine) or the command starts it ('cd $CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/missions/<id>/worktree && nohup npm run dev > /tmp/dev.log 2>&1 & sleep 8; echo http://localhost:5173'). When null, the orchestrator skips user-flow testing and writes verdicts.<fid>.user_test = 'skipped'. mcp names which MCP server drives the browser; v1.1 supports 'playwright' (mcp__playwright__*). 'glance' is planned for v1.2. fail_on_console_errors and fail_on_failed_requests default to false — console errors and 4xx/5xx requests are recorded in the verdict but don't gate the PASS/FAIL (real apps have boot-time third-party noise that would cause spurious retries). Flip to true only when your app's console is genuinely expected to be clean. Note: v1.0 does not auto-stop dev servers the command starts — that's the user's responsibility (in v1.2 we may add preview_server_stop_command).
_mission_runtime_comment: Where the worker and scrutiny execute. worktree.enabled (default true) makes each mission run inside its own git worktree at <mission_root>/<mission_id>/worktree on branch mission/<mission_id> — source-file edits stay isolated from the main checkout and other missions. base_ref names the ref the mission branches from (default 'HEAD' = main repo's current commit; can be 'main', a tag, or a SHA). Set worktree.enabled to false for host mode (worker edits in the main checkout, no isolation). Container isolation is planned for v1.1.
_github_comment: GitHub integration for missions. auto_pr_on_completion (default false) makes the orchestrator push the mission branch and run `gh pr create` automatically at mission completion — the orchestrator never merges, even with this flag (merge is always human). Per-mission override available via /mission --auto-pr flag. Requires the `gh` CLI to be installed and authenticated; the orchestrator does NOT install it for you. /mission --from-issue <url> always works regardless of this flag; it only affects the completion-time PR creation.
